Author: Brenda Jo Bryan
Title: How All This Pervasive AI Slop Released Me from Perfectionist Prison
Text:
Member-only story
How All This Pervasive AI Slop Released Me from Perfectionist Prison
Generative AI is forcing us to have a deeper appreciation for flawed human-generated writing.
The more I read complaints about AI-generated writing being impersonal, lacking depth, and sounding robotic, the more validated I feel as a writer — even as I know my writing is terrible.
AI-generated writing, as it is now, may be perfect for technical writing, but it’s missing the element of connection that is required for humans to fully grasp an idea or concept.
That element of connection is required because our brains are wired to pay attention to that which matters to us and to ignore everything else. And as a social species, we are programmed to form personal connections. Therefore, when we are connecting with someone, whatever that person talks about becomes relevant to us. When someone we care about is talking to us about information we need, we pay extra attention to it, due to the necessity of the information and building a connection.
It is one reason why learning is so much easier when we study with or learn from fellow humans — the most efficient being in person and when it is interactive. It is also why ads and sales copies that sell feelings and emotions work so well: they tap into…
